View in browser

Understanding V8 Hidden Classes

JavaScript engines like V8 use hidden classes (also called shapes or maps) to optimise property access on objects. When you create objects with the same properties in the same order, V8 can reuse the same hidden class, enabling it to use fast inline caches instead of hash lookups.

The practical consequence is that you should avoid adding properties to objects after construction, and you should initialise all properties in the constructor rather than conditionally later. This single habit can meaningfully reduce memory pressure and improve throughput in hot loops.

Benchmarks show that objects with stable shapes run property access up to 10x faster than those where shape transitions occur frequently. In real-world applications the gains are typically more modest, but still worthwhile in performance-critical code paths.

Dev Digest is published every Friday.
